title: Substack's New AI Detector Has the Same Blind Spot DEV.to's Did
excerpt: Substack shipped an AI detector this week. Every post, note, and comment over 100 words can now be...
content_html:
Substack shipped an AI detector this week. Every post, note, and comment over 100 words can now be scanned through Pangram to see how much of it reads as human or AI. Chris Best's launch post frames it as giving readers a choice: the platform isn't banning AI use, just surfacing it. Worth knowing going in: Pangram's own data already ranks Substack as the cleanest of the platforms it scans, a fraction of LinkedIn's AI-content rate. They're launching transparency tooling from the platform with the least to hide.

I read that and thought: I already know exactly how this goes. I lived it on a smaller scale, with a person instead of a platform integration.

A few months ago I got flagged twice in one day by "Sloan," DEV.to's moderation-warning system. Not a bot quietly scoring posts in the background. A specific community member, reading articles and running them through GPTZero, then sending the same message a blunt classifier would have sent.

The two pieces that got flagged were the ones that generated the most technical discussion I'd published all year. Short paragraphs. Named data points. Rhetorical questions doing real work. The features that make an argument land are the same features that read as "AI-shaped" to anyone calibrated to notice them, human or model.

Write worse, look more human. Write well, get flagged.

That thread also surfaced the part nobody had a clean answer for: the policy creates a dishonesty incentive. Two equally AI-assisted pieces, equally good — the one with a disclosure gets flagged, because now there's something to catch. The one without doesn't. The system was catching transparency, not AI use.

And then Marco showed up in the comments. Forty years in tech, writing in his second language, using AI to make sure his Italian didn't flatten into something stiffer than he meant. Same Sloan message. Same classifier verdict. Nothing to do with what the policy was built for.

Pangram is a real classifier with real engineering behind it: hard negative mining against its own false positives, training data deliberately mirrored so it can't just learn "formal writing = AI." That's more rigor than one guy running GPTZero between article reads. I'll give it that.

But it inherits the same structural problem Sloan had, because it's answering the same narrow question: does this text look AI-shaped. Not: did a human do the thinking. Chris Best's own post admits as much. Pangram can't tell you whether care went into something, only whether the sentences pattern-match to a machine's output.

That gap is where Marco lives. Detectors trained without deliberately mirrored data have a documented habit of flagging non-native English writing, since careful, formal phrasing correlates with both AI output and someone translating in their head before they type — enough of a problem that several major universities have stopped letting instructors use AI detectors at all. Pangram claims their mirror-prompt method fixes it. Maybe. Most of the numbers backing that claim trace back to Pangram or a study Pangram commissioned.

Someone with no stake in the answer already looked. The Atlantic's Matteo Wong traced a recent wave of AI-writing accusations back to Pangram itself, including a horror novel pulled from a major publisher days before its release. His argument wasn't that the tool is broken. It's that a detector that's mostly reliable can be more dangerous than one that's obviously unreliable, because people stop checking. A 99.98% accuracy rate sounds like certainty. Applied across millions of posts, the failures are still real people, still real reputations, just quieter about it.

That's Marco's risk, and mine, in one sentence: the false positive doesn't feel like a statistic when it's your byline.

I write from Port Harcourt, in English, the language I was taught in and think in, using AI as part of an actual workflow: not to generate opinions I don't have, but to get from a rough draft to a clean one without losing the argument along the way. Sloan already showed me what a false positive costs, close enough that I don't need to imagine it. Marco is the version of that risk I can't unsee.

It's also the whole reason I stopped using a generic humanizer and built one calibrated to my own published corpus instead. A tool trained to strip "AI-shaped" patterns from anyone's writing will also strip the parts of your writing that are just yours, an em dash you use structurally, a habit of compressing three examples into two. Voice-humanizer checks against what I actually sound like, not against a mirrored dataset of nobody in particular.

Sloan and Pangram are both answering "does this look like AI." I don't think that's the question that matters. The question is whether someone can be asked "did you know what you were writing about, and do you stand behind it," and answer yes.

I do.

Chris Best's launch post frames it as giving readers a choice: the platform isn't banning AI use, just surfacing it. sentences.zh: 现在可以通过 Pangram 扫描每篇超过 100 个单词的帖子、注释和评论，看看其中有多少是人类或人工智能读取的。

sentences.en: Chris Best's launch post frames it as giving readers a choice: the platform isn't banning AI use, just surfacing it.

sentences.en: Two equally AI-assisted pieces, equally good — the one with a disclosure gets flagged, because now there's something to catch.
sentences.zh: 两篇同样由人工智能辅助的作品，同样出色——其中有披露内容的作品会被标记，因为现在有一些东西需要捕捉。

sentences.en: The one without doesn't.
sentences.zh: 没有的则没有。

sentences.en: The system was catching transparency, not AI use.
sentences.zh: 该系统关注的是透明度，而不是人工智能的使用。

sentences.zh: 然后马可出现在评论中。

sentences.zh: 四十年的科技生涯，用他的第二语言写作，使用人工智能来确保他的意大利语不会变得比他想象的更生硬。

sentences.en: Same Sloan message.
sentences.zh: 同样的斯隆信息。

sentences.en: Same classifier verdict.
sentences.zh: 相同的分类器判决。

sentences.en: Nothing to do with what the policy was built for.
sentences.zh: 与制定政策的目的无关。

sentences.en: Pangram is a real classifier with real engineering behind it: hard negative mining against its own false positives, training data deliberately mirrored so it can't just learn "formal writing = AI."
sentences.zh: Pangram 是一个真正的分类器，背后有真正的工程：针对自身的误报进行硬负挖掘，刻意镜像训练数据，这样它就不能仅仅学习“正式写作 = 人工智能”。

sentences.en: That's more rigor than one guy running GPTZero between article reads.
sentences.zh: 这比在阅读文章之间运行 GPTZero 更严格。

sentences.en: I'll give it that.
sentences.zh: 我会给它。

Chris Best's own post admits as much. Pangram can't tell you whether care went into something, only whether the sentences pattern-match to a machine's output.

sentences.en: But it inherits the same structural problem Sloan had, because it's answering the same narrow question: does this text look AI-shaped.
sentences.zh: 但它继承了斯隆所面临的相同结构性问题，因为它回答了相同的狭隘问题：这段文本看起来是人工智能形状的吗？

sentences.en: Not: did a human do the thinking.
sentences.zh: 不是：是人类思考的吗？

sentences.en: Chris Best's own post admits as much.
sentences.zh: 克里斯·贝斯特（Chris Best）自己的帖子也承认了这一点。

sentences.en: Pangram can't tell you whether care went into something, only whether the sentences pattern-match to a machine's output.
sentences.zh: Pangram 无法告诉你是否关心某件事，只能告诉你句子是否与机器的输出模式匹配。

blocks.html_en: That gap is where Marco lives. Detectors trained without deliberately mirrored data have a documented habit of flagging non-native English writing, since careful, formal phrasing correlates with both AI output and someone translating in their head before they type — enough of a problem that several major universities have stopped letting instructors use AI detectors at all. Pangram claims their mirror-prompt method fixes it. Maybe. Most of the numbers backing that claim trace back to Pangram or a study Pangram commissioned.

sentences.en: That gap is where Marco lives.
sentences.zh: 那个缺口就是马可居住的地方。

sentences.en: Detectors trained without deliberately mirrored data have a documented habit of flagging non-native English writing, since careful, formal phrasing correlates with both AI output and someone translating in their head before they type — enough of a problem that several major universities have stopped letting instructors use AI detectors at all.
sentences.zh: 在没有刻意镜像数据的情况下训练的检测器有一个记录在案的习惯，即标记非母语英语写作，因为仔细、正式的措辞与人工智能输出和打字前在头脑中进行的翻译相关——这个问题已经够多了，以至于几所主要大学已经完全停止让教师使用人工智能检测器。

sentences.en: Pangram claims their mirror-prompt method fixes it.
sentences.zh: Pangram 声称他们的镜像提示方法可以解决这个问题。

sentences.en: Maybe.
sentences.zh: 或许。

sentences.en: Most of the numbers backing that claim trace back to Pangram or a study Pangram commissioned.
sentences.zh: 大多数支持这一说法的数字都可以追溯到 Pangram 或 Pangram 委托进行的一项研究。

blocks.html_en: Someone with no stake in the answer already looked. The Atlantic's Matteo Wong traced a recent wave of AI-writing accusations back to Pangram itself, including a horror novel pulled from a major publisher days before its release. His argument wasn't that the tool is broken. It's that a detector that's mostly reliable can be more dangerous than one that's obviously unreliable, because people stop checking. A 99.98% accuracy rate sounds like certainty. Applied across millions of posts, the failures are still real people, still real reputations, just quieter about it.

sentences.en: Someone with no stake in the answer already looked.
sentences.zh: 与答案无关的人已经看过了。

sentences.en: The Atlantic's Matteo Wong traced a recent wave of AI-writing accusations back to Pangram itself, including a horror novel pulled from a major publisher days before its release.
sentences.zh: 《大西洋月刊》的 Matteo Wong 将最近一波人工智能写作指控追溯到 Pangram 本身，其中包括一本在发行前几天被一家主要出版商撤下的恐怖小说。

sentences.en: His argument wasn't that the tool is broken.
sentences.zh: 他的论点并不是工具坏了。

sentences.en: It's that a detector that's mostly reliable can be more dangerous than one that's obviously unreliable, because people stop checking.
sentences.zh: 一个最可靠的探测器可能比一个明显不可靠的探测器更危险，因为人们停止了检查。

sentences.en: A 99.98% accuracy rate sounds like certainty.
sentences.zh: 99.98% 的准确率听起来像是板上钉钉的事情。

sentences.en: Applied across millions of posts, the failures are still real people, still real reputations, just quieter about it.
sentences.zh: 应用于数以百万计的帖子中，失败仍然是真实的人，仍然是真实的声誉，只是更加安静。

blocks.html_en: That's Marco's risk, and mine, in one sentence: the false positive doesn't feel like a statistic when it's your byline.

sentences.en: That's Marco's risk, and mine, in one sentence: the false positive doesn't feel like a statistic when it's your byline.
sentences.zh: 简而言之，这就是 Marco 的风险，也是我的风险：当你署名时，误报感觉不像是一项统计数据。

blocks.html_en: I write from Port Harcourt, in English, the language I was taught in and think in, using AI as part of an actual workflow: not to generate opinions I don't have, but to get from a rough draft to a clean one without losing the argument along the way. Sloan already showed me what a false positive costs, close enough that I don't need to imagine it. Marco is the version of that risk I can't unsee.

sentences.en: I write from Port Harcourt, in English, the language I was taught in and think in, using AI as part of an actual workflow: not to generate opinions I don't have, but to get from a rough draft to a clean one without losing the argument along the way.
sentences.zh: 我在哈科特港用英语写作，这是我学习和思考的语言，使用人工智能作为实际工作流程的一部分：不是为了产生我没有的意见，而是从草稿到清晰的草稿，同时又不丢失论点。

sentences.en: Sloan already showed me what a false positive costs, close enough that I don't need to imagine it.
sentences.zh: 斯隆已经向我展示了误报的成本，非常接近，我不需要想象它。

sentences.en: Marco is the version of that risk I can't unsee.
sentences.zh: 马可就是那种我无法忽视的风险的版本。

blocks.html_en: It's also the whole reason I stopped using a generic humanizer and built one calibrated to my own published corpus instead. A tool trained to strip "AI-shaped" patterns from anyone's writing will also strip the parts of your writing that are just yours, an em dash you use structurally, a habit of compressing three examples into two. Voice-humanizer checks against what I actually sound like, not against a mirrored dataset of nobody in particular.

sentences.en: It's also the whole reason I stopped using a generic humanizer and built one calibrated to my own published corpus instead.
sentences.zh: 这也是我停止使用通用人性化工具并构建一个根据我自己发布的语料库进行校准的人性化工具的全部原因。

sentences.en: A tool trained to strip "AI-shaped" patterns from anyone's writing will also strip the parts of your writing that are just yours, an em dash you use structurally, a habit of compressing three examples into two.
sentences.zh: 一个经过训练可以从任何人的写作中剥离“人工智能形状”模式的工具，也会剥离你写作中属于你自己的部分，你在结构上使用的破折号，将三个例子压缩成两个的习惯。

sentences.en: Voice-humanizer checks against what I actually sound like, not against a mirrored dataset of nobody in particular.
sentences.zh: 语音人性化器会根据我的实际声音进行检查，而不是针对任何人的镜像数据集。

blocks.html_en: Sloan and Pangram are both answering "does this look like AI." I don't think that's the question that matters. The question is whether someone can be asked "did you know what you were writing about, and do you stand behind it," and answer yes.

sentences.en: Sloan and Pangram are both answering "does this look like AI."
sentences.zh: 斯隆和 Pangram 都在回答“这看起来像人工智能吗”。

sentences.en: I don't think that's the question that matters.
sentences.zh: 我认为这不是重要的问题。

sentences.en: The question is whether someone can be asked "did you know what you were writing about, and do you stand behind it," and answer yes.
sentences.zh: 问题是是否可以问某人“你知道你在写什么吗？你支持它吗？”并回答“是”。

content_html_zh:
Substack 本周发布了一款 AI 探测器。 现在可以通过 Pangram 扫描每篇超过 100 个单词的帖子、注释和评论，看看其中有多少是人类或人工智能读取的。 克里斯·贝斯特 (Chris Best) 的发布帖子将其定义为给读者一个选择：该平台并没有禁止人工智能的使用，只是将其浮出水面。 值得一提的是：Pangram 自己的数据已将 Substack 列为其扫描的最干净的平台，仅占 LinkedIn 人工智能内容率的一小部分。 他们正在从平台上推出透明度工具，几乎不需要隐藏。

我读了之后想：我已经清楚地知道这是怎么回事了。 我以较小的规模生活，与人而不是平台集成。

几个月前，我一天内被 DEV.to 的审核警告系统“Sloan”标记两次。 不是一个机器人在后台悄悄地对帖子进行评分。 特定的社区成员，阅读文章并通过 GPTZero 运行它们，然后发送与钝分类器发送的相同消息。

被标记的两篇文章是我全年发表的最具技术性讨论的文章。 短段落。 命名数据点。 反问句做实际工作。 引起争论的特征与任何被校准来注意到它们的人（人类或模型）视为“人工智能形状”的特征相同。

写得更糟糕，看起来更人性化。 写得好，就被标记。

这条线索还浮现了无人能给出明确答案的部分：该政策创造了不诚实的激励。 两篇同样由人工智能辅助的作品，同样出色——其中有披露内容的作品会被标记，因为现在有一些东西需要捕捉。 没有的则没有。 该系统关注的是透明度，而不是人工智能的使用。

然后马可出现在评论中。 四十年的科技生涯，用他的第二语言写作，使用人工智能来确保他的意大利语不会变得比他想象的更生硬。 同样的斯隆信息。 相同的分类器判决。 与制定政策的目的无关。

Pangram 是一个真正的分类器，背后有真正的工程：针对自身的误报进行硬负挖掘，刻意镜像训练数据，这样它就不能仅仅学习“正式写作 = 人工智能”。 这比在阅读文章之间运行 GPTZero 更严格。 我会给它。

但它继承了斯隆所面临的相同结构性问题，因为它回答了相同的狭隘问题：这段文本看起来是人工智能形状的吗？ 不是：是人类思考的吗？ 克里斯·贝斯特（Chris Best）自己的帖子也承认了这一点。 Pangram 无法告诉你是否关心某件事，只能告诉你句子是否与机器的输出模式匹配。

那个缺口就是马可居住的地方。 在没有刻意镜像数据的情况下训练的检测器有一个记录在案的习惯，即标记非母语英语写作，因为仔细、正式的措辞与人工智能输出和打字前在头脑中进行的翻译相关——这个问题已经够多了，以至于几所主要大学已经完全停止让教师使用人工智能检测器。 Pangram 声称他们的镜像提示方法可以解决这个问题。 或许。 大多数支持这一说法的数字都可以追溯到 Pangram 或 Pangram 委托进行的一项研究。

与答案无关的人已经看过了。 《大西洋月刊》的 Matteo Wong 将最近一波人工智能写作指控追溯到 Pangram 本身，其中包括一本在发行前几天被一家主要出版商撤下的恐怖小说。 他的论点并不是工具坏了。 一个最可靠的探测器可能比一个明显不可靠的探测器更危险，因为人们停止了检查。 99.98% 的准确率听起来像是板上钉钉的事情。 应用于数以百万计的帖子中，失败仍然是真实的人，仍然是真实的声誉，只是更加安静。

简而言之，这就是 Marco 的风险，也是我的风险：当你署名时，误报感觉不像是一项统计数据。

我在哈科特港用英语写作，这是我学习和思考的语言，使用人工智能作为实际工作流程的一部分：不是为了产生我没有的意见，而是从草稿到清晰的草稿，同时又不丢失论点。 斯隆已经向我展示了误报的成本，非常接近，我不需要想象它。 马可就是那种我无法忽视的风险的版本。

这也是我停止使用通用人性化工具并构建一个根据我自己发布的语料库进行校准的人性化工具的全部原因。 一个经过训练可以从任何人的写作中剥离“人工智能形状”模式的工具，也会剥离你写作中属于你自己的部分，你在结构上使用的破折号，将三个例子压缩成两个的习惯。 语音人性化器会根据我的实际声音进行检查，而不是针对任何人的镜像数据集。

斯隆和 Pangram 都在回答“这看起来像人工智能吗”。 我认为这不是重要的问题。 问题是是否可以问某人“你知道你在写什么吗？你支持它吗？”并回答“是”。

I do.
